result:
**Goal: Add a Butterfly Effect mode — fork any running simulation, apply a single-cell perturbation, and visualize the divergence between the original and altered timelines as a real-time spreading heatmap overlay.**

This would let users pause any simulation, mark a "what-if" point, flip one cell, then watch both timelines run in parallel with a color-coded divergence map showing exactly how and where the perturbation cascades. It ties together the existing timeline/bookmark infrastructure, the phase transition detector, and the computation detector into a powerful causal analysis tool — turning the simulator into something that can answer "did this cell actually matter?" for any mode.

Shall I build it?
